entity.summary: Bitcoin wallet, payment-processing, brokerage, and exchange-like service founded around 2012. Coinapult restored service after a March 2015 hot-wallet compromise and continued through later banking and wallet integrations, but independent wallet tracking marked the service no longer operational by December 2018; HEI retains inactive status because no formal shutdown notice establishes an exact terminal date.
entity.notes: Coinapult was primarily a wallet, broker, payment, and liquidity service rather than a conventional order-book exchange. The 2015 hack and restoration are already represented in canonical history. Current coinapult.com content is repurposed and is not treated as continuity of the original service.

expected.summary: Bitcoin wallet, payment-processing, brokerage, and exchange-like service founded around 2012 by Ira Miller and Erik Voorhees. Coinapult suffered a 150 BTC hot-wallet compromise in March 2015 and restored services afterward. Later wallet-directory references mark the service inactive / no longer operational, so HEI models it as inactive rather than dead.
expected.notes: Coinapult is included as an exchange-like historical bitcoin wallet / brokerage / payment service rather than a conventional order-book exchange. HEI keeps it inactive because current references indicate the original wallet service is no longer operational, but the evidence set does not establish a clean official shutdown announcement or terminal date. Current coinapult.com content appears repurposed and should not be treated as proof of original service continuity.

evidence.title: Full Service Restored
evidence.notes: First-party confirmation that Coinapult restored wallet and service operations after the 2015 security incident.

evidence.title: Coinapult Wallet review and inactive status
evidence.notes: Wallet directory states that Coinapult Wallet was no longer operational by December 2018, supporting inactive status without proving one exact shutdown day.
